title: Call: An investor has a long position in a stock that...
summary: Calculate the price of a European call option using the Black-Scholes model.
prompt: An investor has a long position in a stock that pays a dividend yield of 4% per annum. If the stock is priced at $100 and the risk-free rate is 5% per annum, calculate the fair value of a European call option with an exercise price of $100 and 1 year until maturity using the Black-Scholes model. Assume the volatility is 20% per annum.
solution:
**Step 1: Define Key Variables** - Stock price (S) = $100, Strike price (K) = $100, Risk-free rate (r) = 5%, Dividend yield (q) = 4%, Volatility (σ) = 20%, Time (T) = 1 year.
**Step 2: Calculate d1 and d2 using Black-Scholes formulas**:
d1 = [ln(S/K) + (r - q + (σ²/2))T] / (σ√T)
d1 = [ln(100/100) + (0.05 - 0.04 + (0.2²/2)) × 1] / (0.2)
d1 = 0.25
d2 = d1 - σ√T = 0.25 - 0.2 = 0.05
**Step 3: Calculate Call Option Price**: Call Price = S e^(-qT) N(d1) - K e^(-rT) N(d2) = 100 e^(-0.04) N(0.25) - 100 e^(-0.05) N(0.05) = $10.45.
